content: Tool-based memory operation can be framed as a built-in curiosity-driven "gatekeeper" that dynamically expands an agent's effective working memory capacity, aligning active learning state-of-the-art advances with solutions to long-standing working memory bottlenecks. This framework creates a new memory management paradigm where the agent proactively offloads or retrieves memory via external tools only when curiosity-driven intrinsic reward signals predict that additional information will improve reasoning accuracy.
